articles.title: ADB will help finance $70 bn worth of projects to improve electricity-sharing, internet access across Asia-Pacific by 2035
articles.description: The Asian Development Bank plans to back $70 billion in energy and digital infrastructure projects across Asia-Pacific by 2035 to improve connectivity and access. Under its power grid initiative, it aims to mobilise $50 billion to link cross-border electricity networks, support renewable energy, and expand transmission infrastructure.

articles.title: 'Raja Shivaji' box office: Riteish Deshmukh's epic rewrites history
articles.description: Riteish Deshmukh's historical epic 'Raja Shivaji' sets a new benchmark in Marathi cinema with a record-breaking opening weekend of Rs 23.90 crore. The film, also directed by Deshmukh, is now the biggest opener ever for a Marathi movie.

articles.title: Hyderabad Man Dragged On Car Bonnet For 2 Km After Argument Over U-Turn; Driver Fled
articles.description: The man was dragged on a car bonnet for 2 km after a road rage clash over a U-turn signal, the driver fled and police have registered a case.

articles.title: Vibrant Gujarat: Russian, Ukrainian delegates join meditation sessions
articles.description: Special meditation sessions at the Vibrant Gujarat Regional Conference in Surat saw participation from Ukrainian and Russian embassy delegates on consecutive days. The free sessions, organized by Sri Aurobindo Integral Life Centre, have drawn 200 guests.

articles.title: Jabalpur boat tragedy: Search ops end as death toll rises to 13
articles.description: Search operations in the Jabalpur boat tragedy at Bargi Dam have concluded with the recovery of two more bodies, bringing the death toll to 13. All missing persons have been accounted for. PM Modi has announced an ex-gratia for the victims' kin.

articles.title: Army officer's wife's organ donation saves 14-year-old boy's life
articles.description: The family of a 41-year-old brain-dead woman, wife of an Indian Army officer, donated her organs. Her heart was successfully transplanted into a 14-year-old boy with end-stage heart failure at Indraprastha Apollo Hospital in New Delhi.

articles.title: Baloch Women Forum on disappearance of two women in Kech district
articles.description: The Baloch Women Forum voiced serious concern over the enforced disappearance of two women from Kech district, stating it spreads "fear and distress." The women were reportedly detained during a late-night raid and their whereabouts are unknown.

articles.description: The NTA is conducting the NEET (UG) 2026 medical entrance exam for over 22.79 lakh registered candidates. The test is being held in pen-and-paper mode across more than 5,400 centres in India and 14 cities abroad.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: BJP candidates seek divine blessings before results
articles.description: Ahead of the West Bengal Assembly election 2026 vote counting, BJP candidates including Purnima Chakraborty and Sital Kapat visited temples to offer prayers. They expressed confidence in winning and forming a government under PM Modi's leadership.

articles.title: Pune Child Rape-Murder Sparks Protests, Highway Blocked Near Navale Bridge
articles.description: A horrific incident from Pune’s Bhor Taluka has sparked widespread outrage after a 4-year-old girl was allegedly raped and brutally murdered. The case came to light after the child went missing in the afternoon, following which her family launched a frantic search. Her body was later discovered, leading to shock and anger in the locality. Police say CCTV footage helped identify and arrest the accused. While the investigation is underway, the incident has triggered massive protests near the Navale Bridge, where villagers and the victim’s family blocked the Pune–Bengaluru highway.

articles.title: 5 Celebrities Tara Sutaria Was Linked To Before Aditya Roy Kapur

articles.title: Why Shah Rukh Khan was banned after Wankhede fight? Ex-ACP breaks silence on 2012 row
articles.description: Shah Rukh Khan’s 2012 Wankhede Stadium controversy is back in the spotlight as fresh claims from a former ACP shed new light on the heated clash that led to his ban. The incident, involving a reported altercation with security staff after an IPL match, had sparked massive debate at the time. Now, years later, the resurfaced version of events is reigniting curiosity among fans, with many questioning what really went down that night and whether the ban was justified.

articles.title: Phone sirens startle millions as government rolls out real-time warning system
articles.description: A nationwide test of a new cell broadcast disaster warning system caused widespread confusion and alarm across India Saturday morning. Phones blared "Extremely Severe Alert" messages, startling commuters, theatre-goers, and patients. While intended to test reach and speed, the drill highlighted the need for clearer communication about such vital emergency alerts.

articles.title: "She has been great to me": Dianna Russini finds unexpected loyalty as longtime friend shares emotional gesture amid Mike Vrabel controversy
articles.description: Dianna Russini’s departure from The Athletic has sparked widespread debate, but strong backing from Jon “Stugotz” Weiner has added a personal dimension to the story. His public job offer and emotional support highlight her relationships within the industry. Meanwhile, Mike Vrabel’s situation continues to draw attention, though Patriots quarterback Drake Maye emphasized locker room unity. The unfolding developments reveal a split between public scrutiny and internal stability across both narratives.

articles.title: Two FIRs against defector MP Pathak? Punjab cops won’t say
articles.description: Two FIRs, reportedly under non-bailable charges, have been filed against Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab, shortly after he switched from AAP to BJP. Political leaders from BJP and SAD have decried the move as "political vendetta" and an attempt to intimidate party members. Security was heightened at Pathak's Delhi residence.
